content: We can frame naturally occurring biological pathogenic agents (such as viruses) as swarm autonomous agents operating with an evolved ICM curiosity mechanism: their intrinsic reward signal comes from discovering novel, unrecognized host cell receptors, driving them to mutate and explore new host niches without external direction. This perspective explains how viruses naturally evolve the ability to cross species barriers, framed as an intrinsic curiosity-driven exploration process rather than just random mutation.
